headline: The last dance — the only THREE men ever to play six World Cups (Messi, Ronaldo, Ochoa)
summary: At WC 2026, three players reach a sixth World Cup — Lionel Messi (Argentina, defending champions), Cristiano Ronaldo (Portugal), and Mexico's goalkeeper Guillermo Ochoa. They are the ONLY three in history to do so; the previous ceiling was five (Lothar Matthäus, Gianluigi Buffon, Rafael Márquez). Because Mexico plays the tournament opener (June 11), Ochoa could be the first of the three to actually take the field at a sixth World Cup. Widely framed as the veteran GOATs' final tournament; Messi is managing a hamstring issue in the build-up.
angles: Three men, six World Cups — Messi, Ronaldo, or Ochoa: whose longevity is most impressive?
Ochoa could be the FIRST ever to play a sixth World Cup — does that beat Messi & Ronaldo?
Is this really the last time we ever see Messi at a World Cup?
